homepage.text_excerpt: Seamus Logan – MP for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East Skip to main content Skip to footer Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Contact MP for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East Welome to the official website of Seamus Logan MP "As your Member of Parliament, my priority is to represent the needs and concerns of our community at every level. Whether it’s standing up for local services, supporting families and businesses, or ensuring your voice is heard in Westminster, I am committed to working tirelessly on your behalf. Every decision I make is guided by one simple principle: putting people first." Contact Me Upcoming Surgeries Latest Constituency News Stay up to date with local constituency news Latest Westminster News Keep informed with the latest Westminster news Staying Informed Welcome to the news and updates section. Here you’ll find the latest statements, press releases, and updates from Seamus Logan MP — keeping constituents informed about local issues, parliamentary work, and community initiatives. Featured News Article Scottish Hauliers Hit Breaking Point due to Westminster ‘Fuel Tax Hike’ The Westminster government’s shambolic handling of fuel prices is pushing haulage firms in North-east Scotland to the brink by volatile costs and a lack of support. The Westminster government is preparing to reintroduce fuel duty in stages from September, which I believe will act like... Read full Article All News Articles 28 April 2026 UK Government has failed to meet WASPI Campaigners Despite Pre-Election Pledges The UK Government has failed to meet with the WASPI campaigners in the last two years… 24 April 2026 Building Workforce Opportunities and Skills in CCUS I’ve given my backing to the Carbon Capture and Storage Association’s skills initiative launched at a… 23 April 2026 Logan Joins New Cross-Poverty Prevention Initiative as Founding Member As one of 20 founding supporters of a new Parliamentary Poverty Prevention Caucus, I’ll be working… 22 April 2026 Challenging Assumptions on Multiple Sclerosis for MS Awareness Week 2026 I’m supporting the MS Society’s campaign to challenge assumptions on Multiple Sclerosis during MS Awareness Week… Contact Seamus All News & updates Before becoming an MP, I was a mental health social worker, working at all levels before retiring in 2013 as regional director. For much of the next decade, I worked as a coach driver and tourist guide across the region. My interest in politics goes back to my student days, but later I was active as a campaigner for a number of politicians in Ireland. I then joined the SNP in 2018, shortly after moving to Fraserburgh, and went on to campaign in local and national elections. This included the 2022 election to Aberdeenshire Council for the Fraserburgh and District Ward. About Seamus I was born in Ballymoney in the 1950s in County Antrim, Northern Ireland, and brought up in Dunloy , a good grounding for living now in the Broch as we call Fraserburgh. I feel a great affinity with the people and communities who live here and I appreciate the welcome I received from them when I first arrived. The Northern Irish have a lot in common with folk from this corner of Scotland – forthright, a wee bit brusque, hard-working, industrious, and imaginative. It’s the people that make it here and I am glad to be one of them now. It’s the people too that are at the heart of everything I do as their MP – I want to put them first, to stand up for them and act as a voice for their concerns at Westminster, no matter party affiliation or none. And I’m well aware of what they have entrusted to me to do as their elected representative. Working hard for the people, every day. From tackling local issues on the ground to shaping national policy, I believe in being a visible and approachable MP. That means listening to constituents, holding regular surgeries, and working alongside community groups, charities, and businesse

pages.title: Scottish Hauliers Hit Breaking Point due to Westminster ‘Fuel Tax Hike’ – Seamus Logan
pages.h1: Scottish Hauliers Hit Breaking Point due to Westminster ‘Fuel Tax Hike’
pages.text_excerpt: Scottish Hauliers Hit Breaking Point due to Westminster ‘Fuel Tax Hike’ – Seamus Logan Skip to main content Skip to footer Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Contact Back to News Scottish Hauliers Hit Breaking Point due to Westminster ‘Fuel Tax Hike’ Posted: 27 April 2026 The Westminster government’s shambolic handling of fuel prices is pushing haulage firms in North-east Scotland to the brink by volatile costs and a lack of support. The Westminster government is preparing to reintroduce fuel duty in stages from September, which I believe will act like a stealth tax on the backbone of the Scottish economy. In my opinion, it’s is a total farce that Scotland – the nation that fills Westminster’s coffers with oil revenues – is being forced to pay some of the highest pump prices in Europe. Local hauliers are telling me they simply cannot plan for next month, let alone next year, because of volatile price fluctuations. The SNP has been clear: We need a Fuel Duty Regulator now. We need the VAT on duty scrapped. Instead, we have a Westminster government that is happy to find money for steelworks in the south of England while standing idly by as the Grangemouth refinery closes its doors. By allowing our refining infrastructure to vanish while hiking taxes on fuel, the Westminster government is effectively sabotaging Scottish industry. I have written to the Minister today to demand they stop the planned duty increases and finally give our hauliers the stability they deserve. Share this post Seamus Logan MP It's the people too that are at the heart of everything I do as their MP – I want to put them first, to stand up for them and act as a voice for their concerns at Westminster, no matter party affiliation or none. I’m aware of what they have entrusted to me to do as their elected representative. Contact Seamus This website is funded from the Parliamentary allowances provided by the taxpayer. pages.title: UK Government has failed to meet WASPI Campaigners Despite Pre-Election Pledges – Seamus Logan
pages.h1: UK Government has failed to meet WASPI Campaigners Despite Pre-Election Pledges
pages.text_excerpt: UK Government has failed to meet WASPI Campaigners Despite Pre-Election Pledges – Seamus Logan Skip to main content Skip to footer Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Contact Back to News UK Government has failed to meet WASPI Campaigners Despite Pre-Election Pledges Posted: 28 April 2026 The UK Government has failed to meet with the WASPI campaigners in the last two years since they were elected and since the Parliamentary Health Service Ombudsman (PHSO) published its report. The PHSO findings entitled ‘Women’s State Pension Age: Our findings on injustice and associated issues’ recommended compensation due to DWP maladministration while the Conservatives were in power at Westminster in March 2024. Pre the 2024 General Election, many Labour MPs and party candidates pledged their support for the WASPI women’s plight, but on taking office as the next UK Government, failed to accept the findings of the PHSO and refused compensation. Subsequently, there is no record of any meeting with WASPI women in an official capacity at Westminster since September 2024. I believe there is a real disconnect between the Government’s warm words for the WASPI campaign pre-election and its lack of action post-election on implementing the recommendations of the PHSO. As has been noted on numerous occasions, it is unprecedented for a UK Government to refuse to accept a Parliamentary Ombudsman’s findings; to add insult to injury, it is disingenuous to infer support for a campaign before voters take to the polls to then reverse your position once in power. Perhaps this is why Ministers at the DWP are so reluctant to meet these campaigners in person. I’m sure it would be very uncomfortable for them to explain face to face to women so badly failed by their government why they should not receive compensation and yet victims of other scandals should. Because Westminster has been plagued by injustices in how members of the public have been mistreated by public bodies in the last few years. Given the Government is finally in the process of acting to compensate those affected by the Infected Blood scandal, the Post Office scandal and now, hopefully the nuclear test veterans as well, it is well beyond time that the Labour government recognise that WASPI women’s need compensation needs addressed too. In Parliament this week in reply to my suggestion for Labour to U-turn on another of their policies turned bad, the Pensions’ Minister highlighted how compensating WASPI women was never an actual Labour manifesto pledge. I fear this makes the UK Government look even more hypocritical in their numerous photo op poses with WASPI campaigners only to back pedal once they could make a real difference in government to the injustice suffered by these 1950s born women. The UK Government need to relocate their backbone, meet with WASPI women, listen to their concerns and suggestions for ways to implement a full and comprehensive compensation scheme as recommended by the PHSO, and make good on the pledges they made so readily before they came to power. Share this post Seamus Logan MP It's the people too that are at the heart of everything I do as their MP – I want to put them first, to stand up for them and act as a voice for their concerns at Westminster, no matter party affiliation or none. I’m aware of what they have entrusted to me to do as their elected representative. Contact Seamus This website is funded from the Parliamentary allowances provided by the taxpayer. It has been prepared to assist Seamus Logan MP in their parliamentary duties. It is not funded for party political purposes. Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Contact Website by UC

pages.title: Logan Joins New Cross-Poverty Prevention Initiative as Founding Member – Seamus Logan
pages.h1: Logan Joins New Cross-Poverty Prevention Initiative as Founding Member
pages.text_excerpt: Logan Joins New Cross-Poverty Prevention Initiative as Founding Member – Seamus Logan Skip to main content Skip to footer Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Contact Back to News Logan Joins New Cross-Poverty Prevention Initiative as Founding Member Posted: 23 April 2026 As one of 20 founding supporters of a new Parliamentary Poverty Prevention Caucus, I’ll be working together with cross party parliamentarians to prevent poverty and reduce inequality. In Aberdeenshire North and Moray East, it is estimated that 2264 households are living on below the average income, underlining the urgency of coordinated action at both a local and national level. The Caucus, supported by Taxpayers Against Poverty in partnership with Compassion in Politics, aims to bring together MPs from across political parties to promote practical, forward-looking solutions that reduce hardship, strengthen communities, and deliver long-term economic value. As part of my commitment, I’ve signed a unified parliamentary pledge: “ I pledge to prioritise policies preventing poverty and inequality, acting with integrity, respect, and compassion in Parliament and my constituency.” The Parliamentary Poverty Prevention Caucus will work alongside parliamentarians, experts and other groups, to advance policies designed to prevent the causes of poverty. A launch event is planned in Westminster later this year, in collaboration with parliamentary groups focused on poverty and compassionate politics. Alongside the parliamentary pledge, Taxpayers Against Poverty has also launched a public petition calling on the government to make tackling priority one of its key priorities. More information here: https://www.taxpayersagainstpoverty.org.uk/stop-funding-failure Share this post Seamus Logan MP It's the people too that are at the heart of everything I do as their MP – I want to put them first, to stand up for them and act as a voice for their concerns at Westminster, no matter party affiliation or none. I’m aware of what they have entrusted to me to do as their elected representative. Contact Seamus This website is funded from the Parliamentary allowances provided by the taxpayer. It has been prepared to assist Seamus Logan MP in their parliamentary duties. It is not funded for party political purposes. Home About News Newsletters Surgeries Contact Website by UC
